metadata.description: Singapore Biology curriculum covering O-Level and JC H2 A-Level, with prerequisite ordering and full subtopic coverage

nodes.label: Cell Organelles
nodes.description: Structure and function of nucleus, mitochondria, chloroplasts, ribosomes, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, centrioles, vacuoles

nodes.label: Plant vs Animal Cells
nodes.description: Comparing plant and animal cells; cell wall, chloroplasts, large central vacuole in plant cells; use of light microscope

nodes.label: Cell Specialisation
nodes.description: Specialised cells (red blood cells, root hair cells, nerve cells); levels of organisation: cell, tissue, organ, organ system

nodes.label: Diffusion
nodes.description: Definition of diffusion; factors affecting rate of diffusion; importance in living organisms; gas exchange surfaces

nodes.label: Osmosis
nodes.description: Osmosis as diffusion of water through a selectively permeable membrane; effects on plant and animal cells; plasmolysis and turgidity

nodes.label: Active Transport
nodes.description: Movement of substances against concentration gradient; role of energy (ATP); examples in root hair cells and gut lining

nodes.label: Biological Molecules
nodes.description: Carbohydrates, fats and proteins; chemical elements in each; building blocks (monosaccharides, amino acids, fatty acids & glycerol)

nodes.label: Food Tests
nodes.description: Testing for starch (iodine), reducing sugars (Benedict's), protein (Biuret), fats (ethanol emulsion); qualitative analysis

nodes.label: Water & Its Properties
nodes.description: Properties of water important for life: solvent, high specific heat capacity, transport medium

nodes.label: Enzyme Structure & Function
nodes.description: Enzymes as biological catalysts; protein nature; active site; lock-and-key hypothesis; specificity

nodes.label: Factors Affecting Enzyme Activity
nodes.description: Effect of temperature, pH, substrate concentration on enzyme activity; denaturation; optimum conditions

nodes.label: Photosynthesis
nodes.description: Word and balanced chemical equation; role of chlorophyll; conversion of light energy to chemical energy in glucose

nodes.label: Leaf Structure & Adaptations
nodes.description: Internal structure of a leaf: palisade mesophyll, spongy mesophyll, stomata, guard cells; adaptations for photosynthesis

nodes.label: Factors Affecting Photosynthesis
nodes.description: Effect of light intensity, CO2 concentration, temperature on rate of photosynthesis; limiting factors; experiments

nodes.label: Mineral Nutrition in Plants
nodes.description: Need for mineral ions: nitrate for amino acids, magnesium for chlorophyll; effects of mineral deficiency

nodes.label: Balanced Diet
nodes.description: Nutrients: carbohydrates, fats, proteins, vitamins, minerals, water, dietary fibre; energy requirements; malnutrition

nodes.label: Digestive System
nodes.description: Alimentary canal: mouth, oesophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine; associated organs: liver, pancreas, salivary glands

nodes.label: Digestive Enzymes
nodes.description: Amylase, protease, lipase; role of bile in emulsification; chemical vs physical digestion; optimum pH of enzymes

nodes.label: Absorption & Assimilation
nodes.description: Absorption in small intestine; villi and microvilli adaptations; assimilation of nutrients; role of liver

nodes.label: Heart Structure & Function
nodes.description: Chambers of the heart; valves; cardiac cycle; coronary heart disease; pacemaker

nodes.label: Blood Vessels
nodes.description: Structure and function of arteries, veins, capillaries; relating structure to function

nodes.label: Blood Composition
nodes.description: Red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets, plasma; functions of each component; blood clotting

nodes.label: Circulatory System
nodes.description: Double circulatory system; pulmonary and systemic circulation; lymphatic system

nodes.label: Xylem & Phloem
nodes.description: Structure and function of xylem and phloem; arrangement in roots, stems, leaves

nodes.label: Transpiration
nodes.description: Transpiration stream; factors affecting rate of transpiration; stomatal control; wilting

nodes.label: Translocation
nodes.description: Transport of sucrose and amino acids in phloem; source to sink movement

nodes.label: Aerobic Respiration
nodes.description: Word and balanced chemical equation; role of oxygen; energy release from glucose; comparison with combustion

nodes.label: Anaerobic Respiration
nodes.description: Anaerobic respiration in muscles and yeast; lactic acid; ethanol and CO2; oxygen debt; fermentation

nodes.label: Gas Exchange in Humans
nodes.description: Structure of the human gas exchange system; lungs, alveoli, diaphragm, intercostal muscles; breathing mechanism

nodes.label: Kidney Structure & Function
nodes.description: Structure of kidney and nephron; ultrafiltration; selective reabsorption; formation of urine

nodes.label: Osmoregulation
nodes.description: Role of ADH in regulating water content of blood; negative feedback; dialysis as treatment for kidney failure

nodes.label: Excretory Products
nodes.description: Excretion of CO2 and urea; difference between excretion and egestion; role of liver in deamination

nodes.label: Nervous System
nodes.description: Central and peripheral nervous system; sensory, relay and motor neurones; synapses; voluntary and involuntary actions

nodes.label: Reflex Arc
nodes.description: Structure and function of a reflex arc; stimulus, receptor, coordinator, effector, response; examples of reflexes

nodes.label: The Eye
nodes.description: Structure of the eye; focusing (accommodation); pupil reflex; long-sightedness and short-sightedness; corrective lenses

nodes.label: Hormonal System
nodes.description: Endocrine glands; insulin and glucagon in blood glucose regulation; adrenaline; comparison of nervous and hormonal control

nodes.label: Thermoregulation
nodes.description: Maintenance of constant body temperature; role of skin in temperature regulation; vasodilation, vasoconstriction, sweating, shivering

nodes.label: Blood Glucose Regulation
nodes.description: Role of insulin and glucagon; negative feedback; diabetes (Type 1 and Type 2); management of diabetes

nodes.label: Tropisms
nodes.description: Phototropism and gravitropism; role of auxins; experiments demonstrating tropisms

nodes.label: Infectious Diseases
nodes.description: Transmission of diseases; pathogens: bacteria, viruses; examples (cholera, influenza, HIV/AIDS); conditions for spread

nodes.label: Body Defences
nodes.description: First line of defence (skin, mucous membranes); phagocytosis; antibody production by lymphocytes; active and passive immunity

nodes.label: Vaccination & Antibiotics
nodes.description: Principles of vaccination; active immunity; role of antibiotics; antibiotic resistance; distinction between antibiotics and antibodies

nodes.label: Mitosis
nodes.description: Stages of mitosis; role in growth, repair and asexual reproduction; significance of maintaining chromosome number

nodes.label: Meiosis
nodes.description: Stages of meiosis; reduction division; significance in producing genetically different haploid gametes; crossing over

nodes.label: Cell Cycle
nodes.description: Interphase (G1, S, G2) and mitotic phase; DNA replication; role of cell cycle in organisms; uncontrolled cell division and cancer

nodes.label: Asexual Reproduction
nodes.description: Binary fission, budding, spore formation; advantages and disadvantages; cloning in plants

nodes.label: Sexual Reproduction in Plants
nodes.description: Structure of a flower; pollination (self and cross); fertilisation; seed and fruit formation; seed dispersal; germination

nodes.label: Human Reproductive System
nodes.description: Male and female reproductive systems; structure and function of testes, ovaries, uterus; puberty and secondary sexual characteristics

nodes.label: Menstrual Cycle
nodes.description: Hormonal control of menstrual cycle; oestrogen and progesterone; ovulation; role of FSH and LH; fertilisation and implantation

nodes.label: Prenatal Development
nodes.description: Development of embryo and fetus; role of placenta; functions of amniotic fluid; birth; methods of contraception

nodes.label: Sexually Transmitted Infections
nodes.description: HIV/AIDS; methods of transmission; prevention and control of STIs

nodes.label: DNA Structure
nodes.description: Double helix structure; nucleotides; complementary base pairing (A-T, G-C); role of DNA in carrying genetic information

nodes.label: Protein Synthesis
nodes.description: Transcription and translation overview; triplet code; role of mRNA; relationship between genes, proteins and characteristics

nodes.label: Genetic Engineering
nodes.description: Principles of genetic engineering; cutting and inserting genes; production of human insulin by bacteria; benefits and risks of GMOs

nodes.label: Mendelian Genetics
nodes.description: Genes and alleles; dominant and recessive; genotype and phenotype; homozygous and heterozygous; Punnett squares

nodes.label: Monohybrid Crosses
nodes.description: Predicting outcomes of monohybrid crosses; test crosses; expected ratios (3:1, 1:1); interpreting genetic diagrams

nodes.label: Codominance
nodes.description: Codominance in inheritance (e.g., sickle cell trait); ABO blood group system as example of multiple alleles and codominance

nodes.label: Sex Linkage
nodes.description: Sex determination (XX/XY); sex-linked inheritance; X-linked conditions (colour blindness, haemophilia); carrier status

nodes.label: Variation
nodes.description: Continuous and discontinuous variation; causes of variation (genetic and environmental); mutation as source of new alleles

nodes.label: Natural Selection & Adaptive Evolution
nodes.description: Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection; survival of the fittest; adaptation; antibiotic resistance as example

nodes.label: Selective Breeding
nodes.description: Artificial selection; advantages and disadvantages; inbreeding; applications in agriculture

nodes.label: Ecosystem Concepts
nodes.description: Habitat, population, community, ecosystem; biotic and abiotic factors; producers, consumers, decomposers

nodes.label: Food Chains & Webs
nodes.description: Trophic levels; food chains and food webs; pyramids of number, biomass and energy; energy loss between trophic levels

nodes.label: Nutrient Cycling
nodes.description: Carbon cycle; water cycle; role of decomposers; nitrogen cycle (basic); recycling of materials in ecosystems

nodes.label: Pollution
nodes.description: Air pollution (sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide); water pollution (sewage, fertiliser runoff, eutrophication); land pollution

nodes.label: Deforestation
nodes.description: Causes and effects of deforestation; loss of biodiversity; soil erosion; impact on carbon cycle

nodes.label: Greenhouse Effect & Climate Change
nodes.description: Greenhouse gases; enhanced greenhouse effect; global warming; effects on ecosystems and sea levels

nodes.label: Conservation
nodes.description: Importance of biodiversity; conservation strategies; sustainable development; role of legislation and education

nodes.label: Water & Inorganic Ions
nodes.description: Hydrogen bonding in water; thermal properties; solvent properties; roles of inorganic ions (Fe2+, PO43-, Ca2+)

nodes.label: Carbohydrates
nodes.description: Monosaccharides (glucose, fructose, galactose); disaccharides (maltose, sucrose, lactose); polysaccharides (starch, glycogen, cellulose); glycosidic bonds

nodes.label: Lipids
nodes.description: Triglycerides; saturated and unsaturated fatty acids; phospholipids; ester bonds; roles of lipids in energy storage and membranes

nodes.label: Proteins
nodes.description: Amino acid structure; peptide bonds; four levels of protein structure (primary, secondary, tertiary, quaternary); globular vs fibrous proteins

nodes.label: Nucleic Acids
nodes.description: Structure of DNA and RNA; nucleotide composition; differences between DNA and RNA; phosphodiester bonds; antiparallel strands

nodes.label: Cell Ultrastructure
nodes.description: Electron microscope view of organelles; nucleus, rough/smooth ER, Golgi apparatus, mitochondria, chloroplasts, ribosomes, lysosomes, cytoskeleton

nodes.label: Endomembrane System
nodes.description: Functional relationship between ER, Golgi, lysosomes, vesicles; protein trafficking and secretion; endosymbiotic theory

nodes.label: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic Cells
nodes.description: Comparing prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell structure; bacteria vs animal/plant cells; viruses as non-cellular

nodes.label: Fluid Mosaic Model
nodes.description: Phospholipid bilayer; integral and peripheral proteins; cholesterol; glycoproteins and glycolipids; membrane fluidity

nodes.label: Membrane Transport
nodes.description: Passive transport (diffusion, facilitated diffusion, osmosis); active transport; carrier and channel proteins; water potential

nodes.label: Endocytosis & Exocytosis
nodes.description: Bulk transport; phagocytosis, pinocytosis, receptor-mediated endocytosis; secretion by exocytosis

nodes.label: Cell Signalling
nodes.description: Signal transduction; ligand-receptor interaction; intracellular signalling pathways; role of membrane receptors

nodes.label: Enzyme Kinetics
nodes.description: Rate of reaction; Michaelis-Menten kinetics; Vmax and Km; enzyme-substrate complex; induced fit model

nodes.label: Enzyme Inhibition
nodes.description: Competitive and non-competitive inhibition; reversible and irreversible inhibitors; allosteric regulation; feedback inhibition

nodes.label: Cofactors & Coenzymes
nodes.description: Role of cofactors (metal ions) and coenzymes (NAD, FAD, coenzyme A); prosthetic groups; activation energy

nodes.label: DNA Structure (H2)
nodes.description: Watson-Crick model; antiparallel strands; major and minor grooves; packaging of DNA with histones; chromosome structure; euchromatin and heterochromatin

nodes.label: DNA Replication
nodes.description: Semiconservative replication; helicase, DNA polymerase, ligase; leading and lagging strands; Okazaki fragments; Meselson-Stahl experiment

nodes.label: Transcription
nodes.description: RNA polymerase; promoter and terminator; template and coding strands; mRNA processing in eukaryotes (capping, polyadenylation, splicing)

nodes.label: Translation
nodes.description: Ribosome structure; tRNA and anticodons; initiation, elongation, termination; codon table; post-translational modifications

nodes.label: The Genetic Code
nodes.description: Properties of genetic code: degenerate, non-overlapping, universal; start and stop codons; reading frames

nodes.label: Lac Operon
nodes.description: Gene regulation in prokaryotes; structural genes, operator, promoter, regulatory gene; inducible system; role of lactose and repressor

nodes.label: Eukaryotic Gene Regulation
nodes.description: Transcription factors; enhancers and silencers; chromatin remodelling; post-transcriptional regulation (alternative splicing); epigenetics (DNA methylation, histone modification)

nodes.label: Dihybrid Crosses
nodes.description: Independent assortment; 9:3:3:1 ratio; predicting outcomes with Punnett squares; test crosses for dihybrid

nodes.label: Gene Linkage & Crossing Over
nodes.description: Linked genes; recombination frequency; chromosome mapping; effect of crossing over on genetic variation

nodes.label: Epistasis
nodes.description: Gene interaction; modified dihybrid ratios (9:3:4, 12:3:1, 9:7); complementary and supplementary gene interaction

nodes.label: Chi-Squared Test
nodes.description: Null hypothesis; observed vs expected ratios; degrees of freedom; interpreting chi-squared values; statistical significance

nodes.label: Pedigree Analysis
nodes.description: Interpreting pedigree diagrams; autosomal dominant/recessive; sex-linked patterns; determining genotypes from pedigrees

nodes.label: Gene Mutations
nodes.description: Point mutations: substitution, insertion, deletion; frameshift mutations; silent, missense, nonsense mutations; sickle cell anaemia

nodes.label: Chromosome Mutations
nodes.description: Changes in chromosome number: aneuploidy (Down syndrome), polyploidy; changes in chromosome structure: deletion, duplication, inversion, translocation

nodes.label: Cancer Genetics
nodes.description: Oncogenes and tumour suppressor genes; multi-step model of cancer; mutagens and carcinogens; p53 gene

nodes.label: Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)
nodes.description: Principles of PCR; denaturation, annealing, extension; Taq polymerase; applications in forensics and diagnosis

nodes.label: Gel Electrophoresis
nodes.description: Separation of DNA fragments by size; restriction enzymes; DNA fingerprinting; applications

nodes.label: Genetic Engineering (H2)
nodes.description: Restriction enzymes and ligase; vectors (plasmids); recombinant DNA; transformation; production of transgenic organisms; gene therapy

nodes.label: Genomics & Bioinformatics
nodes.description: DNA sequencing; Human Genome Project; comparative genomics; applications of bioinformatics; ethical considerations of genetic technology

nodes.label: Glycolysis
nodes.description: Glucose to pyruvate; phosphorylation; net yield of ATP and NADH; occurs in cytoplasm; substrate-level phosphorylation

nodes.label: Link Reaction
nodes.description: Pyruvate to acetyl CoA; decarboxylation and dehydrogenation; occurs in mitochondrial matrix; production of NADH and CO2

nodes.label: Krebs Cycle
nodes.description: Acetyl CoA enters cycle; production of NADH, FADH2, ATP, CO2; decarboxylation and dehydrogenation reactions; occurs in mitochondrial matrix

nodes.label: Oxidative Phosphorylation
nodes.description: Electron transport chain; chemiosmosis; ATP synthase; role of oxygen as final electron acceptor; proton gradient across inner mitochondrial membrane

nodes.label: Anaerobic Respiration (H2)
nodes.description: Alcoholic fermentation in yeast; lactate fermentation in muscles; comparison with aerobic respiration; respiratory quotient

nodes.label: Light-Dependent Reactions
nodes.description: Photosystems I and II; photophosphorylation (cyclic and non-cyclic); photolysis of water; production of ATP and NADPH; thylakoid membrane

nodes.label: Calvin Cycle
nodes.description: Carbon fixation by RuBisCO; reduction of GP to G3P; regeneration of RuBP; uses ATP and NADPH from light reactions; occurs in stroma

nodes.label: Limiting Factors in Photosynthesis
nodes.description: Effect of light intensity, CO2 concentration, temperature on rate; compensation point; interaction of limiting factors

nodes.label: Mitosis (H2)
nodes.description: Detailed stages (prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase); role of spindle fibres; cytokinesis; significance in growth and repair

nodes.label: Meiosis (H2)
nodes.description: Meiosis I and II in detail; independent assortment; crossing over; chiasmata; sources of genetic variation; comparison with mitosis

nodes.label: Cell Cycle Control
nodes.description: Cell cycle checkpoints (G1, G2, M); cyclins and CDKs; role of p53; apoptosis; loss of control leading to cancer

nodes.label: Natural Selection (H2)
nodes.description: Variation, overproduction, competition, differential survival and reproduction; directional, stabilising, disruptive selection

nodes.label: Evidence for Evolution
nodes.description: Fossil record; comparative anatomy (homologous structures); molecular evidence (DNA/protein sequences); biogeography

nodes.label: Speciation
nodes.description: Allopatric and sympatric speciation; reproductive isolation mechanisms; adaptive radiation; convergent evolution

nodes.label: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
nodes.description: Allele and genotype frequencies; Hardy-Weinberg equation (p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1); conditions for equilibrium; factors causing deviation

nodes.label: Genetic Drift
nodes.description: Random changes in allele frequency; bottleneck effect; founder effect; significance in small populations

nodes.label: Taxonomy & Systematics
nodes.description: Hierarchical classification; binomial nomenclature; three-domain system (Archaea, Bacteria, Eukarya); phylogenetic trees

nodes.label: Species Concept
nodes.description: Biological species concept; morphological species concept; difficulties in defining species; ring species

nodes.label: Biodiversity
nodes.description: Species diversity, genetic diversity, ecosystem diversity; measuring biodiversity (Simpson's index); importance of biodiversity

nodes.label: Threats to Biodiversity
nodes.description: Habitat destruction; invasive species; overexploitation; pollution; climate change as threat to species

nodes.label: Conservation Biology
nodes.description: In-situ and ex-situ conservation; protected areas; captive breeding; seed banks; international agreements (CITES)

nodes.label: Climate Change
nodes.description: Evidence for climate change; greenhouse gases and enhanced greenhouse effect; impact on ecosystems; mitigation and adaptation strategies

nodes.label: Population Ecology
nodes.description: Population growth curves (exponential, logistic); carrying capacity; density-dependent and density-independent factors; r- and K-selected species

nodes.label: Community Ecology
nodes.description: Interspecific relationships: predation, competition, mutualism, parasitism; ecological niches; succession; keystone species

nodes.label: Energy Flow in Ecosystems
nodes.description: Gross and net primary productivity; energy transfer between trophic levels; ecological pyramids; energy budgets

nodes.label: Nutrient Cycling (H2)
nodes.description: Carbon cycle in detail; nitrogen cycle (nitrogen fixation, nitrification, denitrification); role of microorganisms; impact of human activity
